title: High-performance syndrome extraction circuits for quantum codes
decoder_description: An automated framework for constructing low-depth, single-ancilla syndrome extraction circuits for arbitrary CSS codes via left/right data-qubit partitioning and optimized non-interleaved CNOT schedules; this is a circuit-construction method rather than a decoder.
error_models: Standard circuit noise with independent failures on idle operations, CNOTs, preparations, and measurements.
headline_claims: Optimized left-right circuits reduce circuit depth and logical failure rates relative to alternative single-ancilla SEC constructions, with up to order-of-magnitude improvements in logical error rate on the benchmark set.; For the gross code, no non-interleaving SEC can achieve circuit distance 12.; For the gross code, no uniformly tiled non-interleaved SEC can achieve circuit distance 11.; An explicit three-colour depth-8 left-right schedule for the gross code is identified and conjectured to attain circuit distance 11.
runtime_scaling: The ranking workflow is designed so that most candidate circuits can be screened using precomputed residual-distance information rather than expensive circuit-level simulations.; Residual-distance profiles are fast but imperfect surrogates; more comprehensive extended-code analysis is reserved for a small number of promising candidates.
limitations: No threshold value is claimed.; The gross-code distance-11 result is a conjecture supported by extensive search rather than a formal proof.; The no-distance-12 proof applies to non-interleaved circuits; interleaved circuits may still achieve circuit distance 12.; Residual-distance profiles do not capture all interactions between residual errors from different checks.; For some codes, denser detector error models produced by LRCs can increase BPOSD decoding time.
missing_fields: performance_claims.thresholds; links.code_repo_urls
